name: Determinism: solo warrior same seed yields identical results
description: Running the solo warrior scenario twice with the same seeds must produce identical scores and game state, confirming engine determinism. If this fails, there is a non-deterministic bug in the engine.

name: Determinism: full balanced party same seed yields identical results
description: Running the full balanced party twice with the same seeds must produce identical results. Tests determinism with multiple hero interactions.

name: All scenarios produce non-negative mean scores
description: Every scenario's runs should have a non-negative total score on average. Negative scores suggest death/retreat penalties are too harsh or scoring rewards are too low.

name: Score total equals sum of sub-components for all scenarios
description: For every run, score.total must equal killScore + bossScore + waveScore + speedBonus - deathPenaltyTotal - retreatPenaltyTotal - timePenaltyTotal. A mismatch indicates a scoring rule bug.

name: All scenarios defeat at least one enemy per run
description: Every run should defeat at least one enemy. If a run ends with zero kills, the combat system or spawn logic may be broken.

name: Victory always implies boss was defeated
description: Whenever a run ends in victory, the bossDefeated flag must be true. If victory occurs without boss defeat, the victory condition logic has a bug.

name: Duo party scores higher than solo warrior
description: A warrior+mage duo should outperform a solo warrior on average. If not, adding a second hero provides no meaningful benefit — check party synergy mechanics.

name: Trio party scores higher than duo
description: A 3-hero party (warrior+mage+cleric) should outperform a 2-hero party on average. Validates that adding a healer/third member provides value.

name: Full balanced party scores higher than trio
description: A 6-hero balanced party should outperform a 3-hero party. Validates that party size continues to provide advantage at larger sizes.

name: Full party defeats more enemies than solo warrior
description: A 6-hero party should kill more enemies than a solo warrior on average. If not, extra heroes are not contributing to combat effectiveness.

name: Easy mode has higher or equal win rate than normal mode
description: The same party on easy difficulty should win at least as often as on normal. If easy has a lower win rate, the difficulty tuning is inverted.

name: Normal mode has higher or equal win rate than hard mode
description: The same party on normal should win at least as often as on hard. If hard has a higher win rate, difficulty scaling is inverted.

name: Balanced party achieves at least 20% victory rate
description: The balanced 6-hero party should be able to win at least 20% of the time on normal difficulty. A lower rate suggests the game is too punishing for a well-composed party.

name: Solo warrior achieves at least some kills
description: A solo warrior should be able to kill enemies even without a party. Zero kills would indicate the solo experience is completely broken.

name: Balanced party defeats at least as many enemies as mono-warrior party
description: Class diversity should provide at least as much combat effectiveness as stacking the same class. If 4x warriors consistently kill more than a 6-hero balanced team, class balance needs work.
